evals.prompt: How does the UI work in this project? I see there's a Streamlit app but I want to understand all the pages, how navigation works, and what each page does.
evals.expected_output: A report covering the Streamlit UI structure: the main app entry point, the pages module, each page's purpose, and how navigation is wired together. Should cite specific files under src/ui/ and key functions.
expectations: Report mentions src/ui/app.py as the main entry point
Report mentions src/ui/pages.py for navigation/pages module
Report identifies at least 4 distinct UI page files
Report explains how page navigation/registration works
Every finding includes a file path and line number citation

evals.prompt: What ML methods are implemented in this repo? I need to understand each algorithm, how they're configured, and where they're defined.
evals.expected_output: A report identifying each ML method (GCN, MLP, Common Neighbors, etc.), their file locations, key classes/functions, and how they fit into the project. Should cite src/methods/ files.
expectations: Report mentions ML method files under src/methods/
Report describes what each method does (not just listing names)
Every finding includes a file path and line number citation
Report references at least 3 distinct method implementation files

evals.prompt: Trace the full data flow in this project — from loading datasets, through training, to evaluation. How does data move through the system?
evals.expected_output: A report tracing data flow end-to-end: how datasets are loaded (src/data/), how training works (src/train.py), how evaluation runs (src/evaluation/), and how results are presented. Should connect the pipeline stages.
expectations: Report covers the data loading stage (mentions src/data/ or data loading code)
Report covers the training pipeline (mentions src/train.py or training code)
Report covers the evaluation stage (mentions src/evaluation/ or evaluation code)
Report connects the stages into a coherent end-to-end flow
Every finding includes a file path and line number citation
